Class GLRenderCanvas

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.graphics.impl.gl.GLRenderCanvas
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, RenderCanvasExt, RenderCanvas, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
Direct Known Subclasses:
GLSubCanvas
public class GLRenderCanvas extends Object implements RenderCanvasExt
An OpenGL render canvas that accepts GLFramebuffers.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from interface RenderCanvas

    ORIGINAL_SIZE

    Description
    GLRenderCanvas(OpenGLContext parent, boolean clearWhite)

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    attachTarget(Framebuffer target)
    Attaches a new framebuffer for the canvas to draw on.
    protected static void
    checkError()
     
    void
    clear()
    Clears the entire surface.
    void
    clip(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Sets the current clipping region to the given rectangular path.
    void
    close()
    Releases any resources associated with this render canvas.
    GLSubCanvas
    createSubCanvas(double width, double height)
    Creates a new sub-canvas that renders into an offscreen buffer.
    void
    drawImage(Image image, double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an image at the specified position with the given size.
    void
    drawLine(double x1, double y1, double x2, double y2)
    Draws a line from one position to another as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape).
    void
    drawOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape).
    void
    drawRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape).
    void
    drawShape(Shape shape)
    Draws the outline of the specified shape using the current stroke color, line width and blend mode.
    protected void
    ensureBufferBound(boolean draw)
     
    protected void
    ensureOpen()
     
    void
    fillOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in RenderCanvas.fillShape(Shape).
    void
    fillRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in RenderCanvas.fillShape(Shape).
    void
    fillShape(Shape shape)
    Draws an outline of the specified shape as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape) and fills it using the current fill color and blend mode.
    void
    flush()
    Flushes all pending operations, if any.
    OpenGLContext
    getContext()
    Returns the parent context of this canvas.
    Point2D
    getDimensions()
    Returns a vector representing the width and height of the canvas area.
    void
    mulTransform(AffineTransform at)
    Multiplies the coordinate system by the specified affine transform matrix.
    void
    resetClip()
    Resets the clipping region to the default (typically the entire rendering surface).
    void
    restore()
    Restores the most recently saved graphics state from the stack.
    void
    rotate(double degrees)
    Rotates the coordinate system around the origin by the specified angle (subsequent drawing operations will be rotated).
    void
    save()
    Saves the current graphics state (transformations, styling, clipping) onto a stack.
    void
    scale(double sx, double sy)
    Scales the coordinate system by the specified factors.
    void
    setAlpha(byte alpha)
    Sets the alpha (transparency) value for all drawing operations.
    void
    setBlendMode(BlendMode mode)
    Sets the blend mode for combining source pixels with destination pixels.
    void
    setEffects(GraphicEffectMap effects)
    Sets the graphic effects map to apply to subsequent drawing operations.
    void
    setFillARGB(int argb)
    Sets the fill color for shape filling operations.
    void
    setIdentity()
    Resets the current graphics state to default values.
    void
    setLineWidth(double width)
    Sets the line width for stroke operations.
    void
    setStrokeARGB(int argb)
    Sets the stroke color for shape outline drawing operations.
    void
    translate(double dx, double dy)
    Translates the coordinate system by the specified amounts (subsequent drawing operations will be offset by (dx, dy)).

      public void drawShape(Shape shape)

    • drawRect

      public void drawRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape).
      Specified by:
      drawRect in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the rectangle
      h - the height of the rectangle
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape)

      y1 - the Y coordinate of the first position

    • drawImage

      public void drawImage(Image image, double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Draws an image at the specified position with the given size.

      If either dimension is set to RenderCanvas.ORIGINAL_SIZE, then the image's respective dimension is used.

      Specified by:
      drawImage in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      image - the image to draw
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the preferred width of the image or RenderCanvas.ORIGINAL_SIZE
      h - the preferred height of the image or RenderCanvas.ORIGINAL_SIZE
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.drawImage(Image, Point2D, Point2D)

    • createSubCanvas

      public GLSubCanvas createSubCanvas(double width, double height)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Creates a new sub-canvas that renders into an offscreen buffer.

      All drawing operations on the returned canvas are isolated and rasterized into a procedural image when closed. The result can be drawn onto the parent canvas via RenderCanvas.submitImage(double, double, double, double).

      This enables:

      • Coherent application of graphic effects
      • Layered rendering
      • Reusable render passes
      Example: 
      try (RenderCanvas layer = canvas.createSubCanvas(200, 200)) {
    layer.setFillArgb(ARGB.RED);
    layer.fillRect(10, 10, 50, 50);
    layer.drawOval(30, 30, 40, 40);
    // If "canvas" has graphic effects, they'll be applied to the whole image, not to each component separately!
    layer.submitImage(100, 100, 200, 200);
}
      Specified by:
      createSubCanvas in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      width - the width of the sub-canvas
      height - the height of the sub-canvas
      Returns:
      a new render canvas targeting an offscreen buffer

    • translate

      public void translate(double dx, double dy)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Translates the coordinate system by the specified amounts (subsequent drawing operations will be offset by (dx, dy)).
      Specified by:
      translate in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      dx - the distance to translate along X axis
      dy - the distance to translate along Y axis

    • rotate

      public void rotate(double degrees)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Rotates the coordinate system around the origin by the specified angle (subsequent drawing operations will be rotated).

      Note for Scratchers: In Scratch, the rotation angle is relative to the upper part of the vertical axis (0 degrees is facing up, 90 degrees is no rotation, etc.). In the context of graphics, for convenience’s sake (and because there's usually no front face like with Scratch's built-in costumes), a different system is used: 0 degrees means no rotation, positive values rotate clockwise and negative values - counterclockwise. Conversion between these systems happens automatically in vanilla classes, but custom renderers need to do this manually.

      Specified by:
      rotate in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      degrees - the angle of rotation in degrees (0 or multiples of 360 mean no rotation)

    • scale

      public void scale(double sx, double sy)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Scales the coordinate system by the specified factors. Subsequent drawing operations will have their coordinates multiplied by (sx, sy).
      Specified by:
      scale in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      sx - the scaling factor for the X axis
      sy - the scaling factor for the Y axis

    • mulTransform

      public void mulTransform(AffineTransform at)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Multiplies the coordinate system by the specified affine transform matrix.
      Specified by:
      mulTransform in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      at - the transform matrix

    • setFillARGB

      public void setFillARGB(int argb)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the fill color for shape filling operations. This color will be used by subsequent calls to RenderCanvas.fillShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Specified by:
      setFillARGB in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      argb - the ARGB color value (see ARGB)
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.fillShape(Shape)
      • RenderCanvas.setBlendMode(BlendMode)
      • ARGB

    • setStrokeARGB

      public void setStrokeARGB(int argb)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the stroke color for shape outline drawing operations. This color will be used by subsequent calls to RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Specified by:
      setStrokeARGB in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      argb - the ARGB color value (see ARGB)
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape)
      • RenderCanvas.setBlendMode(BlendMode)
      • ARGB

    • setLineWidth

      public void setLineWidth(double width)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the line width for stroke operations. This width will be used by subsequent calls to RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Specified by:
      setLineWidth in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      width - the line width in coordinate units (must be positive)

    • setAlpha

      public void setAlpha(byte alpha)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the alpha (transparency) value for all drawing operations. This value is combined with the alpha components of fill/stroke colors. A value of 0 means fully transparent, 255 (-1) means fully opaque.
      Specified by:
      setAlpha in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      alpha - the alpha value (0-255 unsigned)
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.setFillARGB(int)
      • RenderCanvas.setStrokeARGB(int)

    • setBlendMode

      public void setBlendMode(BlendMode mode)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the blend mode for combining source pixels with destination pixels. The blend mode affects all drawing operations including shape filling/drawing and image drawing.
      Specified by:
      setBlendMode in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      mode - the blend mode to use
      See Also:
      • BlendMode
      • RenderCanvas.drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double)
      • RenderCanvas.fillShape(Shape)
      • RenderCanvas.drawShape(Shape)

    • setEffects

      public void setEffects(GraphicEffectMap effects)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the graphic effects map to apply to subsequent drawing operations.

      Note: Spatial distortion effects can't be applied to vector content (shapes, text); effects that can be applied to them are: HUE_SHIFT, BRIGHTNESS and FADE. Use RenderCanvas.createSubCanvas(double, double) and RenderCanvas.submitImage(double, double, double, double) to apply spatial distortion to vector elements.

      Specified by:
      setEffects in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      effects - the effect map

    • clip

      public void clip(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Sets the current clipping region to the given rectangular path.
      Specified by:
      clip in interface RenderCanvas
      Parameters:
      x - X coordinate of top-left corner
      y - Y coordinate of top-left corner
      w - width
      h - height
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.resetClip()
      • RenderCanvas.translate(double, double)
      • RenderCanvas.rotate(double)
      • RenderCanvas.scale(double, double)

    • resetClip

      public void resetClip()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Resets the clipping region to the default (typically the entire rendering surface). After calling this method, all drawing operations will not be clipped (except by the bounds of the rendering surface itself).
      Specified by:
      resetClip in interface RenderCanvas
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.clip(double, double, double, double)

    • save

      public void save()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Saves the current graphics state (transformations, styling, clipping) onto a stack. The state can be restored later using RenderCanvas.restore().
      Specified by:
      save in interface RenderCanvas
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.restore()
      • RenderCanvas.translate(double, double)
      • RenderCanvas.rotate(double)
      • RenderCanvas.scale(double, double)

    • restore

      public void restore()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Restores the most recently saved graphics state from the stack. This reverts all transformations, styling, and clipping to their values when RenderCanvas.save() was last called.
      Specified by:
      restore in interface RenderCanvas
      See Also:
      • RenderCanvas.save()

    • setIdentity

      public void setIdentity()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Resets the current graphics state to default values. Useful for finer control over texture/primitive position, rotation, scale, etc.
      Specified by:
      setIdentity in interface RenderCanvas

    • getDimensions

      public Point2D getDimensions()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Returns a vector representing the width and height of the canvas area.
      Specified by:
      getDimensions in interface RenderCanvas
      Returns:
      the dimensions of this canvas

    • getContext

      public OpenGLContext getContext()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Returns the parent context of this canvas.
      Specified by:
      getContext in interface RenderCanvas
      Returns:
      the parent context of this canvas

    • close

      public void close()
      Description copied from interface: RenderCanvas
      Releases any resources associated with this render canvas. After calling this method, the canvas should not be used. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RenderCanvas

    • ensureBufferBound

      protected void ensureBufferBound(boolean draw)

    • checkError

      protected static void checkError()

    • ensureOpen

      protected void ensureOpen()
